dartpijl.monkeyIqAria: Monkey IQ {{percentile}} percent — you beat {{percentile}} percent of the monkeys
dartpijl.compareAria: You {{userPoints}} points against the average monkey {{monkeyAvg}} points
dartpijl.quote: Ah well, the Tour, that's a lottery, isn't it…

kopman.label: Team leader of the peloton — the monkey drops on the first climb.

kopgroep.label: In the breakaway, ahead of the monkeys.

schouder.label: Shoulder to shoulder with the monkey in the pack.

gelost.label: The monkey with the dart rides you off its wheel.

dist.srSummary: You beat {{beaten}} of the {{total}} monkeys; the average monkey scores {{mean}} points.

explainer.title: The monkey with the dart — explained
explainer.p1: It feels like you have to predict the best riders. But cycling is uncertain: crashes, form of the day, weather and team tactics all help decide who wins. No one controls that.
explainer.p2: The monkey with the dart makes that visible. It blindly throws its darts in every category and then also picks two random jokers. Such a gamble sometimes turns out surprisingly well — not because the monkey knows anything, but because chance plays a big role in cycling.
explainer.whatSimBody: We let {{count}} monkeys assemble such a random team and score each team with exactly your points system. We line up your score against those {{count}} monkey scores. That is your percentile: you beat X% of the monkeys.
explainer.band1: ≥ 85%: hats off — this is clearly skill, not luck.
explainer.band2: 60–84%: solid — you're above the average of chance.
explainer.band3: 40–59%: a draw — skill and luck balance each other out.
explainer.band4: < 40%: the monkey would have done better; bananas for you.
explainer.outro: Even with perfect knowledge, many outcomes remain possible. One grand tour is just a snapshot, so luck plays a bigger role than you might think. So treat the results mainly as a fun indication, not as an absolute truth.

dartpijl.emptyTitle: The monkeys are still warming up 🐒
dartpijl.emptyMessage: As soon as the first stage result is in, the monkeys throw their darts and you'll see how you do against them.
dartpijl.top10Commentary: "Top 10% of the monkeys — that dart of yours has vision." 🔥

peloton.title: Peloton picks
peloton.subtitle: Am I following the peloton here or going my own way? Dark = rare pick, light = peloton favourite.

directorAnalysis.lineSafe: Your peloton picks what everyone picks. A safe bidon, no fireworks.
directorAnalysis.lineRisky: With these differentials you're aiming either for the podium or the broom wagon.
directorAnalysis.lineUniques: {{count}} riders barely anyone picked. Nerve or madness?
directorAnalysis.quote0: Today your squad would probably get dropped on the first col.
directorAnalysis.quote1: Your squad breathes: all-in on chaos.
directorAnalysis.quote2: Taking four sprinters into these mountains? Ambitious tactics.
directorAnalysis.quote3: The peloton trusts Pogačar. You trust hope.
directorAnalysis.quote4: This squad has the organisation of an early break in a rainy stage.

fallback.s90: Chapeau. Your peloton rides on the front and the simulation monkeys see only your back wheel. That's how you do it. I couldn't have arranged it better myself.
fallback.s80: Strong riding. You're ahead of the pack and the dart monkey sits in the team car. Dare a touch more and you take the win.
fallback.s70: Sound work so far. You score above the median, your riders are earning their wages. We'll speak again in the mountains.
fallback.s60: It's respectable, but I'm watching closely. Your squad delivers, only the margin is thin. No reason to shine, every reason to keep pedalling.
fallback.s50: Midfield, and frankly it feels that way too. The monkeys are riding on your wheel. Not lost, but you're not yet sprinting for the win.
fallback.s40: Tough course for your squad. The monkeys are doing just as well and your standing is under pressure. Sit up straight and recover, come on.
fallback.s00: Hard race. The figures don't lie, and I lie even less. You're losing on every front. The broom wagon rides behind you, but quitting isn't in my vocabulary.

wielerdirecteur.title: De Wielerdirecteur
wielerdirecteur.previewHeader: Patlef's preview — a taster until the race begins
wielerdirecteur.previewError: Patlef is keeping his powder dry for now.
wielerdirecteur.reportLoading: "Patlef is at his typewriter. Your report is coming — and he's not going easy on you…"
wielerdirecteur.clickOpen: Open a component for the calculation.
wielerdirecteur.emptyTeam: Build a team first — then the director will happily have a word.

info.title: How is the score calculated?
info.intro: The report score runs from <mono>1.0</mono> to <mono>10.0</mono> (minimum 3.0) and is made up of four weighted components.
info.poolBody: Your position in the pool relative to all other participants. Rank 1 gives the maximum contribution, last place the minimum.
info.monkeyBody: The percentage of 10,000 random simulation teams that you beat.
info.jokerBody: Return on your jokers: how many points they scored relative to the best possible jokers in the field. Jokers that yield nothing drag the sub-score down.
info.diffBody: Daring that pays off. You earn points for riders who <bold>both</bold> scored points <bold>and</bold> were picked by few others. Per scoring rider his points count, weighted by <mono>(1 − picked%)</mono>: a rider picked by 10% weighs 0.9×, one picked by 80% only 0.2×. The (points-weighted) average of these is your sub-score. Lots of points with rarely-picked riders → towards 10. Only popular favourites → lower.

calc.poolNote: N = number of participants · rank = your position
calc.monkeyNote1: The app simulates 10,000 random teams ("monkeys" picking riders and two jokers at random) and counts how many of them score fewer points than you. beatPct = that percentage.
calc.monkeyNote2: Point allocation: 0% beaten → 0.0 · 50% → 0.5 · 100% → 1.0. This sub-score weighs 25%. Example: 72% of monkeys beaten → monkeyScore 0.72 → sub-grade (0.72×9+1)=7.5/10.
calc.jokerNote: bestJokerPoints = 2 top-scoring non-category riders · no jokers → 0.5
calc.diffNote1: Only riders who scored points count. "picked%" = share of participants who picked that rider. Rarely picked → counts more heavily. No scoring picks → 0.5.
calc.diffNote2: Example: rider A 40 pts, 10% picked → contribution 40×0.9=36. Rider B 30 pts, 70% picked → 30×0.3=9. diffScore = (36+9)/(40+30) = 45/70 = 0.64 → sub-grade 6.8/10.

detail.pool: N = number of participants ({{total}}), rank = your position (#{{rank}}). The higher you stand, the higher the sub-score.
detail.monkey: You beat {{pct}}% of 10,000 random simulation teams ("monkeys" picking riders and two jokers at random).
detail.jokerNote: bestJokerPoints = the 2 top-scoring riders who are in no category.
detail.diffIntro: Per scoring rider: <mono>contribution = points × (1 − picked%)</mono>. Rarely picked counts more heavily. <green>green</green> ≤25% · <red>red</red> ≥60%.
detail.subGradeNote: The sub-score (0–1) is converted to the 1–10 scale with ×9+1, which is why 0.63 → 6.7 and not 6.3.

emirates.explain1: The dream team consists of the top-scoring riders per category. Stage points are awarded 50 points for place 1, 40 for place 2, down to 1 point for place 20.
emirates.explain2: On top of that come two jokers: the two best remaining riders.
emirates.emptyBody: As soon as the jury updates the first stage result, the dream team unfolds here.

uitleg.metaDescription: Discover everything you can do in Koerspoule: L'Équipe, Volgwagen, Subpoule, Results and Hors Catégorie — briefly explained, with direct links.
uitleg.title: How it works
uitleg.intro: Everything you can do in Koerspoule, briefly explained. Click a card to go straight there.
uitleg.lequipeIntro: Everything about the latest stage in one place, refreshed every day.
uitleg.lequipeBullet1: The commentary by Michel Wuyts and José De Cauwer on the result in your subpoule.
uitleg.lequipeBullet2: The report from your directeur sportif (Patrick Lefevère) on your team.
uitleg.lequipeBullet3: Your subpoule's stage result, with each participant's haul.
uitleg.lequipeBullet4: The preview of the next stage: profile, route, type and distance.
uitleg.lequipeBullet5: A shortcut to the standings with the results of all participants.
uitleg.lequipeNote: The commentary and the report appear once the stage result has been entered. Until then you'll see the preview of the upcoming stage here.
uitleg.cardPloegIntro: Your full team as a race programme, grouped the way you picked it.
uitleg.cardPloegBullet1: At the top, the golden strip "Hunt for yellow" with your GC riders.
uitleg.cardPloegBullet2: Below that your other categories, each with your chosen rider(s); your jokers sit in their own category (✨).
uitleg.cardPloegBullet3: Next to each rider you'll see his points.
uitleg.cardPloegBullet4: A rider who abandoned is crossed out with ☠.
uitleg.cardPloegBullet5: Click a rider for his results per stage, including any joker bonus.
uitleg.cardPronoIntro: Your predictions for the final classifications and jerseys.
uitleg.cardPalmaresIntro: Your palmarès and the results you've bagged in this poule.
uitleg.cardSubKlassementIntro: The standings within your subpoule, with a duel mode to compare teams.
uitleg.cardVerloopIntro: Who's climbing and who's slipping across the stages.
uitleg.cardDaguitslagIntro: The stage result per participant for the latest stage. You can filter by home town, provided you've filled yours in.
uitleg.cardHeatmapIntro: Per category, who went with the crowd and who picked an outsider.
uitleg.cardStreekIntro: In subpoules with home towns, towns battle each other. Only visible when there's a regional classification — that is, when you've filled in your home town.
uitleg.cardUitKlassementIntro: The overall standings of all participants, not just your subpoule.
uitleg.cardEtappesIntro: The stage result per stage for all participants.
uitleg.cardDartpijlIntro: Luck counts: 10,000 monkeys pick a complete team including two jokers, and your score is measured against them. Read the full explanation via the info button there.
uitleg.cardPelotonkeuzesIntro: See per category who's popular and who dared to differ.
uitleg.cardWielerdirecteurIntro: A directeur sportif reviews your team in a short report.
uitleg.cardEmiratesIntro: The dream team in hindsight: the best possible selection of the day.
uitleg.cardBenchmarkIntro: Compare two teams category by category.
uitleg.faq1Q: How do I create a subpoule?
uitleg.faq1A: Go to the Subpoule tab and click Create subpoule. Pick a name and then share the invite code with your friends. You can create one for as long as the race isn't finished.
uitleg.faq2Q: How do I join a subpoule?
uitleg.faq2A: Ask the creator of the subpoule for the invite code. Go to the Subpoule tab, choose Join and enter the code. Got an invite link? Then the code is already filled in for you.
uitleg.faq3Q: Can I be in multiple subpoules at the same time?
uitleg.faq3A: Yes. You have one team, and it automatically counts in every subpoule you're a member of.
uitleg.faq4Q: How do I change my team name?
uitleg.faq4A: In the Volgwagen, on the My Team tab: click the pencil ✏️ next to your team name. The name is saved immediately and visible in all standings.
uitleg.faq5Q: Until when can I submit my team?
uitleg.faq5A: Until the start of the first stage. If you haven't resubmitted your changes, your current selection automatically counts at that moment.
uitleg.faq6Q: Can I still change my team after submitting?
uitleg.faq6A: Yes, until the start of the first stage. Adjust your team and submit again to confirm.
uitleg.faq7Q: Why don't I see any commentary in my subpoule yet?
uitleg.faq7A: The commentary appears once the stage result has been entered, and is written from 2 participants per subpoule.
uitleg.faq8Q: What happens when a rider abandons?
uitleg.faq8A: Riders who abandon stay in your team, crossed out. You keep the points they already scored.
uitleg.faq9Q: Where do I see the results of all participants?
uitleg.faq9A: In Results: the standings and the stage result per stage, for all participants and not just your subpoule.

rules.jsonldA1: Create a free account with your team name, build your team by picking 1 rider per category plus 2 jokers, predict the GC podium and the jerseys, and challenge your friends via a subpoule code.
rules.jsonldQ2: How do you earn points in the koerspoule?
rules.jsonldA2: Only riders from your selection who finish in the top 20 of a stage earn stage points. Jokers count double. On top of that you earn points for a correct GC podium (max 150) and for correctly predicting the green, polka dot and white jerseys (25 points per jersey).
rules.jsonldQ3: When does my entry need to be in?
rules.jsonldA3: Your entry must be in before the start of the first stage. Changes after that are no longer possible.
rules.jsonldQ4: What happens if a rider leaves the race?
rules.jsonldA4: If a rider from your selection abandons (DNS/DNF) they get 0 points and you can't replace them. Tough luck — that's bike racing.
rules.title: How to play & Rules
rules.quote: "The jury, consisting of Patrick Lefairplay, is right. And if not — still right."
rules.rule1: The jury, consisting of Patrick Lefairplay, is right.
rules.rule3: Your entry must be in before the start of the first stage.
rules.rule4: Build your dream team on the Build Your Team page and put your best riders on the start line.
rules.rule5: If someone leaves the race, tough luck :(
rules.step1Desc: Register with an email address and pick a team name.
rules.step2Body: Pick 1 rider from each of <bold>{{n}}</bold> categories. Add 2 Jokers (free pick outside the categories). Jokers must be unique riders and may not appear among your chosen category riders.
rules.step2Empty: Categories will be made available by the admin soon.
rules.step3Desc: Predict the podium of the final classification + the winners of the points, mountains and young rider jerseys.
rules.step4Desc: Create a subpoule or enter a code to join an existing poule.
rules.perStageDesc: Only riders in your selection who finish in the top 20 earn points. Riders who don't finish (DNS / DNF) get 0 points.
rules.schemaEmpty: No points scheme set by the admin yet.
rules.podiumRow1: Right rider in the right spot (1, 2 or 3)
rules.podiumRow2: Right rider in the top 3, but wrong spot
rules.podiumNote: Each position is judged separately. A rider can earn points at most once. A maximum of 150 points in total for the GC podium.
rules.jerseyGreen: Correct winner of the points jersey (green)
rules.jerseyMountain: Correct winner of the mountains jersey
rules.jerseyYoung: Correct winner of the young rider jersey (white)
rules.jokersDesc: Pick two Jokers from the remaining riders not listed in the categories.
rules.totalDesc: A player's total is the sum of all stage points plus the prediction points earned (podium + jerseys). The standings are updated automatically after each processed stage. The standings after the final stage are the definitive final result.
rules.tttDesc: During the team time trial the stage result is determined by the result of the individual riders, and thus by the standings after day 1. So it's not guaranteed that all 8 riders of the winning team fill places 1 through 8 in the result. If riders drop off earlier, their personal time counts.
rules.dayPrizeDesc: If multiple daily prize winners finish on the same number of points, we draw lots among the winners to decide who takes the daily prize.
rules.categoriesIntro: Pick 1 rider per category + 2 free Jokers.
rules.categoriesEmpty: No categories set for this race yet.
rules.categoriesEmptyNote: The admin will set these before the start of the race.
rules.finallyP1: This koerspoule was built with care and enthusiasm. The goal is simple: getting even more enjoyment out of following the race together 🚴
rules.finallyP2: Want to help us keep improving this koerspoule and keep it running? You can, via the <supportBtn>"Steun Koerspoule"</supportBtn> button. All support is hugely appreciated!

legal.title: Privacy & terms
legal.privacyIntro: Koerspoule respects the privacy of all users and ensures that the personal information you provide to us is treated confidentially.
legal.privacyCollect3: Home town — optional. In some subpoules you can enter your home town for the regional classification. This is not required and your home town is only visible within that subpoule.
legal.privacyCollect4: Technical data such as IP address and browser type (for statistics)
legal.privacyUseTitle: What do we use this data for?
legal.privacyUse1: Providing and improving the Koerspoule service
legal.privacyUse2: Communication about your account and the game
legal.privacyUse3: Keeping track of scores and standings
legal.privacyShare: We do not sell your data and do not share it with third parties for their own purposes. We do use external services (processors) that process data on our behalf to keep the site running — see the section <anchor>External services</anchor>. Beyond that, we only share data when legally required to do so. You have the right at any time to access, correct or have your data deleted. To do so, contact us via <mail>koerspoule@gmail.com</mail>.
legal.cookiesIntro: Koerspoule uses cookies to make the website function properly and to improve your experience.
legal.cookiesFunctional: Functional cookies: necessary for the site to work (e.g. login status, session information).
legal.cookiesAnalytics: Analytical cookies: to gain anonymous insight into how the website is used (e.g. page visits).
legal.cookiesAds: Advertising cookies (Google AdSense): we show advertisements via Google AdSense. Your consent for this is handled by Google's certified consent window (Funding Choices / Privacy & messaging), which only places personalised advertising cookies after you give consent.
legal.cookiesClosing: Functional cookies are always active; advertising cookies only with your consent via the Google window. You can block cookies via your browser settings, but this may affect how the site works.
legal.verwerkersIntro: To make Koerspoule work, we use a number of external services that process data on our behalf. Only the data needed for the relevant function is shared with these parties:
legal.verwerkersSupabase: database, accounts/login and storage of your game data.
legal.verwerkersCloudflare: sending of emails (confirmation and notification emails).
legal.verwerkersOpenai: automatically generating editorial texts (stage commentary and previews).
legal.voorwaardenIntro: By participating in Koerspoule you agree to the following terms:
legal.voorwaarden1: Koerspoule is a free game with no profit motive. There are no financial obligations attached to participation.
legal.voorwaarden2: Participants are themselves responsible for building their team before the stated deadline.
legal.voorwaarden3: The organisation reserves the right to adjust the rules during the season, provided this is communicated in a timely manner.
legal.voorwaarden4: Koerspoule is not liable for any technical failures or loss of data.
legal.voorwaarden5: Inappropriate behaviour in the chat or towards other participants may lead to exclusion.
legal.avgIntro: Koerspoule acts in accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (AVG/GDPR). This means:
legal.avg1: We only process data that is necessary for the game.
legal.avg2: Your data is stored securely and kept no longer than necessary.
legal.avg3: You have the right to access, rectify, transfer and delete your personal data.
legal.avg4: Forms only collect the minimum information required.
legal.avg5: We show advertisements via Google AdSense; the associated advertising cookies are only placed after you give consent.
legal.avgContact: For questions or requests regarding your data, you can contact us via <mail>koerspoule@gmail.com</mail>.
legal.adsGooglePartner: Google uses cookies to show ads based on your prior visits to Koerspoule and other websites. You can manage personalised ads in your Google account settings. Read more about how Google uses data in the <partner>Google privacy policy for partner sites</partner>.

actie.title: Actievoorwaarden Koerspoule
actie.s1Body: These promotion terms apply to the prizes and prize promotions of Koerspoule, offered via koerspoule.nl. Koerspoule is operated by Koerspoule B.V., established in Utrecht. By participating in a poule with prizes attached, you agree to these terms.
actie.s2Body: Participation is open to anyone with a valid Koerspoule account. Participants under the age of 16 need permission from a parent or guardian. Employees of Koerspoule B.V. and parties involved in the organisation are excluded from winning prizes. Each person may participate with one account; multiple accounts to increase the odds of winning are not allowed.
actie.s3Body: The prizes to be won are listed per game and, where applicable, per subpoule on the Prizes page. Prizes may include, among other things, a classification prize, daily prizes and honourable placements, and are partly made possible by sponsors. The prizes shown apply for as long as the relevant promotion runs; Koerspoule reserves the right to replace a prize with an equivalent alternative should a prize unexpectedly be unavailable.
actie.s4Body: Winners are determined based on the final standings and the daily results as calculated in the poule, according to the race rules. Koerspoule's result is binding. No correspondence will be entered into about the result, except in the case of demonstrable errors in the scoring.
actie.s5Body: Winners are announced after the relevant period has ended and are notified via the email address linked to their account. A winner must respond within 30 days of being notified to claim their prize; if a winner does not respond within that period, the claim to the prize lapses. Prizes are personal and not transferable or exchangeable for cash, unless stated otherwise.
actie.s6Body: To award a prize, Koerspoule may request additional details from the winner (such as name and address). These details are used solely for awarding and sending the prize and are processed in accordance with our <privacyLink>Privacy policy</privacyLink>.
actie.s7Body: Koerspoule is not liable for damage arising from participation, from being unable to award a prize due to circumstances beyond its control, or from the use of a prize. Sponsors are responsible for the prizes they make available.
actie.s8Body: Koerspoule reserves the right to amend these terms, and to end or adjust a promotion, without stating reasons. In cases not covered by these terms, Koerspoule decides. These terms are governed by Dutch law.
actie.contact: Questions about the prizes or these terms? Get in touch via <mail>koerspoule@gmail.com</mail>.
